# Runbook — RFQ send daemon (`rfq_send`) (an RFQ row stuck HELD / blocked / "contamination") (Successor-Remediation, Op Stds §43)

<!-- BEGIN TREE-XREF (generated by scripts/build_runbook_xrefs.py — do not edit inside) -->
> **In the troubleshooting tree** ([printable guide](../troubleshooting/troubleshooting_guide.md) · dashboard `/troubleshoot`). This runbook resolves:
>   - Purchase order — build, config, pull/render/file, send → rfq-send transmits an approved request-for-quote to its vendor → “An approved RFQ is not sent.”
> Daemon(s): `rfq-send` — see the [daemon reference](../references/daemon_reference.md).
<!-- END TREE-XREF -->

A §43 successor-remediation entry for the **Successor-Operator** (a trained operator who runs
Claude Code and reads Smartsheet rows + alert emails, not code). The §42 code-reader
rationale lives in the docstrings of `po_materials/rfq_send.py` (the `_VendorRecipientLookup`
+ `_RfqEnvelope` + `_RfqQuoteFormAttachment` bindings) and `po_materials/rfq_send_poll.py`
(the thin poller over `send_poll_core`).

`po_materials/rfq_send.py` is the **send half of the External Send Gate** (FM v11 Invariant 1)
for the **vendor** audience — the outbound half of the RFQ round trip (the inbound half is the
vendor-estimate importer + the R4 form auto-bind). It is the RFQ instantiation of the shared
send engine (`safety_reports.weekly_send.send_one_row`) — the SAME dispatch logic as safety
weekly-send / progress-send / po-send / subcontract-send, a different `SendConfig`. The RFQ
email carries **TWO attachments**: the price-free **RFQ PDF** + the vendor's fillable
**`.xlsx` quote form** (the R3 sequence-attachment seam). The poller
`po_materials/rfq_send_poll.py` (launchd `org.solutionsmith.its.rfq-send`, every 15 min)
discovers approved `RFQ_Pending_Review` rows, runs the **F22** approval-attestation gate
against the **ITS — Purchase Orders** workspace (§46 — the SAME procurement approver set as
POs), stamps the verified approver, then dispatches each to the sender. The sender refuses to
transmit a half-formed, wrong-lane, or numberless RFQ — it marks the row `Send Status = HELD`
instead. From `procurement@`. Recipients resolve at send time from `ITS_Vendors` by Vendor
Key, never the display columns.

**This lane SHIPS DARK.** Go-live — flipping `po_materials.rfq_send.polling_enabled` true AND
`install.sh load org.solutionsmith.its.rfq-send` — is a **FIXED high-capability-class
External-Send-Gate decision → Seth**, never a Successor-Operator action.

> **The both-rule (FM v11 / Op Stds §44).** A fault is Tier-2-eligible (the Successor-Operator
> may self-repair) only if it is **documented here AND low-capability-class**. Anything
> touching the **External Send Gate, secrets/auth, doctrine, or code** is FIXED high-class →
> escalate to Seth regardless of documentation.

> **The External Send Gate boundary — non-negotiable.** The Successor-Operator **NEVER**
> bypasses the F22 gate, **NEVER** edits the approval columns (`Send Now` / `Approve for
> Scheduled Send` / `Approved By` / `Approved At`), and **NEVER** enables a send path. To
> re-send a HELD row the operator fixes the *underlying data* (a vendor email, a Notes tag)
> and re-approves the row through the normal checkbox flow — never marks a row SENT or forces
> a send.

## Symptom A — an RFQ row stuck HELD `held_no_recipient` (blank / unknown vendor email)

### What it means

The sender could not resolve a TO address for the row's **Vendor Key** (which rides the
"Job ID" protocol column on this WSR-schema-twin sheet). Either the Vendor Key is **unknown**
in `ITS_Vendors`, or the vendor's **Contact Email** cell is **blank**. Recipients are read
LIVE from `ITS_Vendors` at each dispatch — the display columns are never used.

### The low-class Tier-2 repair (a data fix)

1. Read the HELD row's **"Job ID"** cell — that is the **Vendor Key** (`VEN-######`).
2. Open `ITS_Vendors`, find the row with that Vendor Key.
   - **No such row** → the key is unknown → a vendor-record / routing question → **escalate to
     Seth** (do not invent a vendor row to force a send).
   - **Row exists but Contact Email blank** → fill the **Contact Email** cell with the correct
     vendor address.
3. Re-send by clearing the hold: set the row's **Send Status** back to `PENDING` and re-check
   the driving approval checkbox. The next poll cycle re-dispatches (the poller skips HELD
   rows, so the hold must be cleared).
4. Confirm the send: the row flips to `SENT` with a `Sent At` stamp.

## Symptom B — an RFQ row stuck HELD `held_missing_envelope` (numberless RFQ)

### What it means

An RFQ email must carry its RFQ number in the subject + attachment name. The number lives in
the row's **Notes** cell as a machine tag (`rfq_id=<n>; rfq_number=<number>; vendor_key=<k>; …`),
seeded by `rfq_poll`. If the Notes lost the `rfq_number=` tag, the envelope can't be built and
the sender HELDs rather than mail a numberless RFQ.

### The low-class Tier-2 repair

1. Look up the RFQ number from **`RFQ_Log`** (or the filed Box PDF) using the row's `rfq_id`
   (in the same Notes cell) or the vendor/project.
2. **Restore the `rfq_number=<number>` tag** in the Notes cell (keep the existing
   `rfq_id=<n>` prefix + the `vendor_key=` tag). The reviewer normally never edits Notes —
   this is a targeted repair of a machine tag, not the approval columns. *Alternative:* cancel
   the RFQ in the portal and re-generate it (a fresh generate re-seeds Notes).
3. Re-send: Send Status → `PENDING`, re-check the approval checkbox.

### Escalate-to-Seth condition

If Notes keep losing the `rfq_number` tag with no operator edit in between → a code defect →
**escalate to Seth**.

## Symptom C — an RFQ row stuck HELD `held_workstream_mismatch` (contamination), and/or CRITICAL `weekly_send.workstream_mismatch`

### What it means

A row on the RFQ review sheet was tagged for a **different** lane than `po_materials_rfq`
(its `Workstream` cell is `po_materials` / `safety` / `subcontracts` / blank-but-wrong). The
sender **blocked the send** (fail-closed) and HARD-HELD the row *before* the write-ahead
SENDING marker. The DISTINCT `po_materials_rfq` tag is what makes cross-lane dispatch
impossible — `po_send` / `subcontract_send` can never dispatch an RFQ row and vice versa. The
guard worked.

### Boundary — FIXED high-capability-class → escalate to Seth

A contamination HELD touches the **External Send Gate**. The Successor-Operator does **NOT**
self-repair it (do not clear the HELD, do not re-tag the row, do not re-approve).

**Read-only checks before escalating:** open the HELD row, read the `Workstream` cell + the
Notes contamination tag, confirm this is the **RFQ** review sheet (`RFQ_Pending_Review`),
change nothing, capture the row ID + the `Workstream` value + the Vendor Key / project.
**Escalate to Seth** — the likely cause is a code/config defect (a row mis-filed onto the RFQ
sheet, or a mis-bound `SendConfig`).

## Symptom D — every RFQ send blocked; F22 `EMPTY_ALLOWLIST` / a wake CRITICAL

### What it means

F22 approver authority for RFQ sends is **membership of the `ITS — Purchase Orders`
workspace** (§46 — the SAME procurement approver set as POs). If no individual approvers are
shared into that workspace, the allowlist is empty and the gate **fails CLOSED** — every send
is blocked. This is correct, not a bug.

### The low-class Tier-2 repair (a workspace-share action)

1. In the Smartsheet UI, share each intended procurement approver **individually** into the
   **`ITS — Purchase Orders`** workspace (the same share list that authorizes PO sends).
2. Sends dispatch on the next cycle.

If you lack workspace-share rights, or are unsure who the approvers should be → **escalate to
Seth** (who-may-approve is a Send-Gate posture question).

## Symptom E — an RFQ row stuck HELD `held_missing_pdf`

### What it means

`rfq_poll` files the price-free RFQ PDF to Box and seeds the "Compiled PDF" link when it
creates the review row. A blank "Compiled PDF" cell → nothing to attach → HELD.

### The low-class Tier-2 repair

1. Confirm the RFQ PDF exists in Box (`<job>/Purchase Orders/RFQs/`).
2. If the Box link is simply missing from the row, restore it in the "Compiled PDF" cell from
   the Box file URL; if the PDF itself was never filed, re-generate the RFQ in the portal (a
   fresh generate re-files the PDF + re-seeds the link).
3. Re-send: Send Status → `PENDING`, re-check the approval checkbox.

## Note — the RFQ arrived to the vendor WITHOUT the quote form (PDF-only)

The `.xlsx` quote form is a **convenience** second attachment; the RFQ PDF is the essential
document, and the vendor-facing body invites a quote "on the attached form OR your own
letterhead". If a vendor reports receiving the RFQ PDF but no form, `rfq_poll` degraded that
vendor's filing to PDF-only (a `rfq_quote_form_failed` WARN in `ITS_Errors`, or the row's
Notes lack a `form_box_file_id=` tag). This is **not** a send failure and needs no re-send —
the vendor can quote on their own letterhead. If EVERY RFQ is missing the form, that is a
systemic render/Box issue → **escalate to Seth** (a code/config question).

## Escalation summary (Tier-2 → Tier-3)

| Situation | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Blank / correctable vendor email, missing Box link, restorable Notes tag | Tier-2 data fix + re-approve |
| Empty F22 approver set (`EMPTY_ALLOWLIST`) | Tier-2 workspace-share into ITS — Purchase Orders |
| `held_workstream_mismatch` / contamination CRITICAL | **Escalate to Seth** (Send-Gate) |
| Unknown Vendor Key, recurring Notes loss, systemic form failure, any code/config cause | **Escalate to Seth** |
| Turning the RFQ send gate ON (go-live) | **Seth only** — FIXED high-class External-Send-Gate flip + plist load |
